items.headline: Anthropic 'Claude Mythos' Leaked — Called a 'Step Change' in Reasoning, Coding & Cybersecurity
items.summary: An internal data leak at Anthropic inadvertently exposed details about an unreleased model codenamed 'Claude Mythos,' described as a watershed moment for cybersecurity. Experts warn it could enable a new wave of agentic AI-driven exploits. Anthropic is privately briefing government officials. Cybersecurity stocks (CrowdStrike, Zscaler) dropped on the news.

items.headline: DeepSeek V4 Will Run Entirely on Huawei Chips — China's AI Independence Milestone
items.summary: DeepSeek's forthcoming V4 model, expected in weeks, will run entirely on Huawei AI chips. Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have already placed bulk orders. This marks a major win for China's push to break free from US chip dependency and signals Huawei's AI chip program is maturing faster than expected.

items.headline: Google Releases Gemma 4 — Open-Weights Models Under Apache 2.0, Built for Agentic AI
items.summary: Google launched a new wave of Gemma 4 open-weights models optimized for agentic AI and coding, now under the more permissive Apache 2.0 license. Aimed squarely at winning enterprise adoption away from Chinese open-weights competitors. Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite also released — 2.5x faster response, 45% better output speed.

items.headline: Anthropic and OpenAI Both Racing to IPO in 2026 — Each Hoping to Beat the Other to Market
items.summary: Axios reports both Anthropic and OpenAI are targeting public offerings in 2026, with each company hoping to beat the other to market. OpenAI also separately announced key nonprofit leadership appointments and committed to investing at least $1B through its nonprofit arm over the next year.

items.headline: US Venture Funding Hits Record $267B — OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI Dominate AI Deals
items.summary: PitchBook reports US venture funding surged to an all-time record of $267B, with AI companies — particularly OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI — accounting for the lion's share of mega-deals. The data underscores how capital is concentrating in frontier AI labs.

items.headline: Anthropic Subscriptions Will No Longer Cover Third-Party Tool Usage Starting Today
items.summary: Anthropic announced Claude subscriptions will no longer cover usage on third-party tools (like OpenClaw) starting April 4 at 12pm PT. The move is to better manage capacity amid surging demand. Users must now pay API costs directly when using Claude through external platforms.

items.headline: Microsoft Deepens Japan Commitment with $10B AI Infrastructure Investment
items.summary: Microsoft announced a $10B investment in Japan spanning 2026-2029, focused on AI infrastructure, cybersecurity partnerships with national institutions, and training over 1 million Japanese engineers and workers by 2030. Part of a broader push to build trusted AI infrastructure outside China.

items.headline: Mark Zuckerberg Back to Writing Code After 20 Years — Heavy Claude Code CLI User
items.summary: Sources say Zuckerberg has submitted three code diffs to Meta's monorepo — his first active coding in two decades — and is described as a heavy Claude Code CLI user. Signals a deeper hands-on AI integration push at the highest levels of Big Tech.

items.headline: Google Gemini Overtakes Perplexity as #2 Web Referral Source
items.summary: March 2026 data shows Google Gemini now accounts for 8.65% of website referrals — up from just 2.31% a year ago — overtaking Perplexity to become the #2 AI-driven referral source behind ChatGPT. AI search is rapidly reshaping web traffic patterns.

items.headline: PrismML Emerges with 1-Bit LLMs; New Open Model Scores #2 on PinchBench at 96% Lower Cost
items.summary: PrismML emerged from stealth with 'Bonsai,' described as the world's first commercially viable 1-bit LLM. Separately, a new open model scored #2 on PinchBench at just $0.90/M output tokens — 96% cheaper than top models — trained on 2,048 NVIDIA B300 GPUs in 33 days. It's now the #1 most-used open model on OpenRouter in the US.

items.headline: March Jobs Report Blows Past Expectations: 178,000 Jobs Added, Unemployment Falls to 4.3%
items.summary: The US economy added 178,000 jobs in March — nearly triple the Dow Jones consensus estimate of 59,000 — the biggest gain in 15 months and the largest since Trump returned to the White House. Unemployment fell to 4.3%. Economists are cautious about the outlook as the Iran war begins weighing on economic confidence.

items.headline: Markets Post First Winning Week Since Iran War Began — S&P +3.4%, Nasdaq +4.4%
items.summary: US markets snapped a 5-week losing streak. The S&P 500 rose 3.4% for the week, the Nasdaq surged 4.4%, and the Dow added nearly 3%. Thursday's session (last before Good Friday market closure) saw the S&P close at 6,582.69. Markets had been under pressure since the US-Israeli strike campaign on Iran began.

items.headline: US Stock Markets Closed Today for Good Friday
items.summary: All US equity markets are closed today (Saturday, April 4) for the Good Friday / Easter holiday weekend. Trading resumes Monday. Markets closed with S&P 500 at 6,582.69, Dow at 46,504.67, Nasdaq at 21,879.18.

items.headline: Oil Surges — WTI Crude Tops $109/Barrel on Iran War Supply Fears
items.summary: WTI crude surged 8.8% above $109/barrel this week as the Iran conflict threatened Middle East oil supply chains. Iran struck Gulf refineries and hit sites in Iraq's Kurdistan region. Energy stocks including CF, OXY, and APA gained 3-5%. Higher oil prices are beginning to ripple through the broader economy via energy costs.

items.headline: Fed's Musalem: No Need to Change Interest Rate Stance Despite Middle East Inflation Risks
items.summary: St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem said Wednesday there is no current need for the Fed to adjust its rate stance, even as rising inflation risks tied to the Iran war mount. Markets are watching closely as oil-driven inflation could complicate the Fed's path on rate cuts.

items.headline: Dow and S&P Posted Worst Quarterly Performance Since September 2022
items.summary: Q1 2026 ended with the Dow and S&P 500 recording their worst quarterly performances since September 2022, driven by the shock of the US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the resulting oil price spike and economic uncertainty. The rebound last week was the first sign of stabilization.

items.headline: Defense Stocks Rally as 'Operation Epic Fury' Intensifies — March Labor Data Shows Surge in Defense Hiring
items.summary: Defense stocks continued their strong run as 'Operation Epic Fury' intensifies in Iran. March labor data specifically showed a surge in defense industry hiring. Stocks of major defense contractors posted gains alongside rising military spending expectations.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Projects S&P 500 to Hit 7,600 by Year-End on 12% EPS Growth
items.summary: Goldman Sachs released a bullish year-end 2026 forecast, projecting the S&P 500 will reach 7,600 driven by 12% earnings-per-share growth. The firm cites a resilient US economy and the 'long-awaited arrival of AI-driven productivity gains' as the key tailwinds — if the Iran conflict doesn't derail the outlook.

items.headline: Wall Street Eyes JPMorgan and Bank of America Q1 Earnings (April 14-15) as Market Litmus Test
items.summary: JPMorgan (April 14) and Bank of America (April 15) Q1 earnings are being called the most important dates on the Q2 calendar — a 'definitive litmus test' for the market's health amid the Iran war, ongoing Fed leadership transition, and AI-driven finance transformation.

items.headline: AI Data Center Build-Out Borrowing Reaches $3 Trillion — Private Markets Face Tense Moment
items.summary: Bloomberg reports the massive $3 trillion borrowing wave to finance AI data center construction is straining private markets, with institutional investors growing cautious about exposure amid rising rates and geopolitical uncertainty. The AI infrastructure build-out continues to be the defining capital market story of 2026.

items.headline: Iran Shoots Down 2 US Military Planes — F-15E Down Inside Iran, Airman Still Missing
items.summary: In the most dramatic escalation of the 5-week US-Israeli war on Iran, Iran shot down two US military aircraft on Friday. An F-15E was downed inside Iran — the first US warplane lost in the conflict — with one crew member missing. A second aircraft crashed near the Strait of Hormuz; that pilot was rescued. US search and rescue operations are ongoing.

items.headline: US-Israeli Strikes Hit Petrochemical Plants and Vicinity of Bushehr Nuclear Site
items.summary: The latest round of US-Israeli strikes hit two petrochemical plants in Iran's Khuzestan province and struck near the Bushehr nuclear power plant, killing one person on the perimeter. Trump had threatened to take Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' and celebrated strikes on a major Tehran highway bridge. Iran vowed escalation.

items.headline: Pentagon Shakeup: 3 Generals Ousted Including Army Chief Amid Iran War Escalation
items.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed the Army's top general and two other senior officers in a sweeping wartime leadership purge, dramatically expanding the Pentagon shakeup as the Iran war intensifies. Critics are questioning command stability as the conflict enters its 5th week with no clear exit strategy.

items.headline: China Positions Itself as Global Diplomatic Leader on Iran — US Appears Uninterested in Cooperation
items.summary: AP reports China is actively seeking a leadership role in Iran war diplomacy, attempting to show global stewardship in contrast to the US's unilateral approach. The Biden era's multilateralism is absent; the Trump administration has shown little interest in international cooperation to end the conflict.

items.headline: Iran Hits Gulf Refineries and Kuwait Airport Drone Attack as War Enters Week 5
items.summary: Iranian proxy and direct attacks struck Gulf oil infrastructure, including refineries, oil storage facilities in Iraq's Kurdistan, and drone strikes at Kuwait International Airport. The attacks are pushing oil prices higher and threatening regional energy supply chains as the conflict shows no sign of resolution.

items.headline: White House Proposes 40% Surge in Military Spending — Defense Budget to Hit $1.5 Trillion
items.summary: The White House proposed a historic 40% increase in military spending, bringing the defense budget to $1.5 trillion — up from roughly $1 trillion. The proposal includes a 5-7% pay raise for servicemembers actively deployed in the Iran war. This would be the largest single-year defense spending increase in modern US history.

items.headline: Trump Proposes 10% Cut to All Other Federal Discretionary Programs to Fund Military
items.summary: To offset the massive defense budget increase, Trump's budget proposes a 10% across-the-board cut to all other federal discretionary spending. This would affect agencies from education to transportation to environmental programs. Congress faces a difficult vote as wartime spending pressures mount.

items.headline: House and Senate Republicans Reach Deal to End Homeland Security Shutdown
items.summary: Congressional Republicans reached a deal to end the partial Homeland Security shutdown, providing some back pay to tens of thousands of employees. TSA officer absenteeism had spiked to 12.4% at its peak; it has since dropped to around 7% as employees began receiving partial payments.

items.headline: Iran War's First American Warplane Loss Raises Political Stakes Domestically
items.summary: The downing of an F-15E and a missing crew member have sparked domestic political fallout. Democrat Rep. Jamie Raskin said the incidents prove the US does not have 'complete domination of the skies' and called for international cooperation to end the war. The incident is fueling growing bipartisan concern about war strategy.

items.headline: Vermont Signs AI Transparency Law — First State to Regulate Synthetic Media in Elections
items.summary: Vermont's S.23 bill regulating the use of AI-generated synthetic media in election campaign material was signed into law, making it one of the first states to enact concrete AI election transparency legislation. The bill passed both chambers after 14 months of deliberation and covers deepfakes and AI-generated political content.

items.headline: Arsenal FA Cup Quarter-Final at Southampton TODAY — Odegaard Could Make Return
items.summary: Arsenal face Southampton in the FA Cup QF at St Mary's today. Martin Odegaard is in contention to return from injury, as is Jurrien Timber. However, Piero Hincapie is out, and both Saka and Rice were sent back from the England camp for medical assessments — a significant injury concern. Arsenal are still smarting from the 2-0 Carabao Cup final loss to Man City.

items.headline: Saka and Rice Sent Back from England Camp for Medical Assessment — Arsenal Injury Concern
items.summary: Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice both departed England training early for Arsenal medical assessments, raising alarm ahead of the FA Cup quarter-final. Noni Madueke was also ruled out with a knee injury from England duty. Eberechi Eze is confirmed out. Arsenal are dealing with a mounting injury crisis at a critical point in the season.

items.headline: Giannis Excoriates Bucks for Sitting Him — NBA Investigation Ongoing
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo publicly blasted the Milwaukee Bucks for keeping him out of games despite his insistence that he is healthy enough to play. An NBA investigation into the situation is continuing. The drama is overshadowing the end of what has been a difficult season for Milwaukee.

items.headline: Celtics Blowout Short-Handed Bucks 133-101 — Milwaukee Loses 5 of Last 6
items.summary: Jaylen Brown (26 pts) and Jayson Tatum (23 pts) led Boston to a dominant 133-101 win over a depleted Milwaukee roster on Friday night. Giannis, Kevin Porter Jr., and Bobby Portis Jr. all sat out. The Bucks have now lost five of their last six games, jeopardizing their playoff position.

items.headline: Packers Enter 2026 NFL Draft With 7 Picks but No First-Round Selection
items.summary: Green Bay enters the April 23-25 draft in Pittsburgh with seven selections but no first-round pick. New defensive coordinator Jonathan Gannon headlines a coaching staff overhaul. GM Gutekunst has been discussing the controversial Rashan Gary trade publicly as the team looks to rebuild certain areas of the roster.

items.headline: Packers' Voluntary Offseason Workouts Start April 20 Under New DC Jonathan Gannon
items.summary: Green Bay's nine-week offseason program kicks off April 20, with coach Matt LaFleur hoping new defensive coordinator Jonathan Gannon drives marked improvement. The NFL also announced the 2026 draft will be held in Pittsburgh, April 23-25.

items.headline: NBA Playoffs Start April 18 — Detroit Pistons Hold East's #1 Seed, Celtics and Knicks Also In
items.summary: The 2026 NBA playoffs begin April 18, with the play-in tournament April 14-17. In the East, the Detroit Pistons hold the #1 seed in a surprising development. The Celtics and Knicks have clinched. In the West, Houston Rockets clinched, and the Thunder, Nuggets, Lakers, and Spurs are rolling into the stretch run.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Leads MVP Race as Thunder Roll to Open April
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has remained at the top of the MVP ladder, continuing to perform at a high level all season. The Thunder demolished the Lakers on Thursday to open April. SGA is currently the league's #1 MVP candidate heading into the final regular season stretch.

items.headline: Liverpool's Alexander Isak Returns from Broken Leg — Fit for FA Cup vs Man City Today
items.summary: Liverpool striker Alexander Isak has returned to training after a broken leg, though it's unclear if he starts against Man City in the FA Cup quarter-final today at the Etihad. Mohamed Salah is fit. Liverpool then face PSG in the Champions League QF on Wednesday — a massive week in English football.

items.headline: NFL Draft in Pittsburgh April 23-25 — Raiders Expected to Take QB Fernando Mendoza #1 Overall
items.summary: The NFL draft is just 19 days away. The Las Vegas Raiders are widely expected to select Indiana quarterback Fernando Mendoza with the #1 overall pick after he won the Heisman Trophy, a national championship, and had a strong Pro Day. Kirk Cousins is reportedly close to a deal with the Raiders as a bridge option.

items.headline: FA Cup QF at Southampton Today — Odegaard & Timber Could Return, Saka/Rice Fitness Doubts
items.summary: Arsenal travel to St Mary's for today's FA Cup quarter-final. Odegaard and Timber are in contention to return from injury. However, Saka and Rice were both sent back from England for medical assessments — their involvement is uncertain. Hincapie is out. Arteta has hinted Raya starts in goal. Arsenal need to refocus after the Carabao Cup final defeat.
items.source: The Athletic / London Standard / Arsenal News

items.headline: Saka, Rice, and Madueke All Depart England Camp Early — Injury Crisis Hits Arsenal
items.summary: Three Arsenal internationals left their national team camps early with injury concerns: Saka and Rice sent back for medical assessment from England, while Noni Madueke suffered a knee injury. Eze is confirmed out. Hincapie (Ecuador) also departed early. This is a significant injury crisis at an absolutely critical moment in Arsenal's season.

items.headline: Arsenal vs Southampton FA Cup QF: Arteta Confirms Raya Starts, Max Dowman in Line
items.summary: Arteta dropped hints in his pre-match press conference that David Raya will start in goal against Southampton, with young midfielder Max Dowman also potentially starting. The Gunners need a strong cup run to salvage something from a season that has seen the Carabao Cup slip away.

items.headline: Arsenal Firmly Reject Man Utd's Interest in Lewis-Skelly — Will Only Sell for 'Absolutely Crazy' Fee
items.summary: Manchester United have been linked with Arsenal's young left-back Myles Lewis-Skelly. Arsenal have sent a clear message that they will not sell for anything less than an 'absolutely crazy' fee, effectively shutting down interest from Old Trafford. Lewis-Skelly is seen as a core long-term asset.

items.headline: Arsenal's West Ham Away Fixture Moved to May 10 for Sky Sports Broadcast
items.summary: Arsenal's trip to West Ham has been rescheduled to Sunday, May 10 after being selected for UK broadcast by Sky Sports. The fixture was originally scheduled for an earlier date. Small logistical item but relevant for the end-of-season fixture run-in.

items.headline: Giannis Blasts Bucks for Sitting Him — Says He's Healthy, NBA Investigation Continues
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo publicly excoriated the Bucks organization for keeping him out of games, insisting he is healthy enough to play. An NBA investigation into whether the team is deliberately sitting him is ongoing. The tension between Giannis and the Bucks front office is boiling over.

items.headline: Bucks Crushed by Celtics 133-101 — Giannis, Porter Jr., Portis All Out
items.summary: A depleted Milwaukee squad was blasted by the Celtics on Friday night with three key players out. The Bucks have now lost 5 of their last 6 games, raising serious questions about their playoff seeding and future direction. Milwaukee needs to stabilize quickly.

items.headline: Bucks Injury Report: Giannis (Knee), Porter Jr. (Knee), Portis (Wrist) All Out vs Boston
items.summary: The Bucks injury report for Friday's game showed Giannis (left knee hyperextension/bone bruise), Kevin Porter Jr. (right knee synovitis), and Bobby Portis Jr. (left wrist sprain) all out. Thanasis Antetokounmpo and Gary Trent Jr. were questionable. Next game vs Memphis on April 5.

items.headline: Doc Rivers Named to Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2026
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks head coach Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame as part of the Class of 2026. Rivers — who won a championship with the Celtics — is being honored for a distinguished coaching career spanning multiple decades and franchises.

items.headline: 2026 Mock Draft Has Bucks Landing Top-3 Pick in Tankathon Simulation
items.summary: A new 2026 NBA mock draft simulation has Milwaukee potentially securing a top-3 pick via their lottery odds, which could reshape the franchise's future — particularly relevant given the growing uncertainty around Giannis's long-term commitment to the team.

youtube.title: AI News: Anthropic Leak is Bigger Than You Think
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt Wolfe's latest AI news breakdown covers the Anthropic 'Claude Mythos' data leak and why it's more significant than initial reports suggest. Also covers other major AI news from the week including new model releases and the broader implications of frontier AI cybersecurity risks.
